description: Massive protests in Brazil sparked by a 20-cent increase in public transport fares in São Paulo. The movement grew to encompass widespread dissatisfaction with corruption and public service failures, becoming the 'June Days'.

key_events.description: Police violence against protesters in São Paulo creates national outrage.
Protests spread to over 100 cities simultaneously.
Government announces the reversal of fare hikes.
Initial public support for protesters dips due to fringe violence and looting reports.

calibration_notes: Illustrates how economic-based protests evolve into broader social unrest and eventually face backlash.
